basis: Anchor 30 plan lists Isshin as a RWB attack-trigger doubling Commander anchor.
EDHREC and public Commander deck pages label Isshin as a Commander deck commander.
The profile targets combat-value Commander, not generic Mardu goodstuff.

source_limit_notes: Aggregate/manual reference only; no copied public decklist.
Attack trigger density and attacker safety are the core product signals.
Do not convert Isshin into Voltron-only or generic Mardu staples by default.

themes.name: attack_trigger_doubling
themes.notes: Isshin doubles attack-triggered abilities and rewards creatures that create value on attack.

themes.name: mardu_go_wide_combat
themes.notes: Token-on-attack and damage-on-attack packages make combat profitable before lethal.

themes.name: extra_combat_payoffs
themes.notes: Extra combat cards are powerful but should not replace attack-trigger density.

avoid_patterns.reason: Green attack-trigger cards can look synergistic but are outside Isshin identity.

examples: equipment pile with few attack triggers
avoid_patterns.reason: Isshin wants attack-trigger density more than commander-damage Voltron.
Go-wide combat decks need protection against board wipes and bad attacks.
